item.title: Codelco, Anglo pursue twin environmental approvals for shared Chile copper pit
item.description: Chilean copper producer Codelco (COBRE.UL) and global miner Anglo American plan to submit separate environmental studies to regulators for their planned shared copper mine in Chile, documents seen by Reuters show, using what they called an "unprecedented" twin-track to streamline the approval process.

item.title: Airflow products maker Madison Air valued at $15.7 billion as shares rise on NYSE debut
item.description: Madison Air Solutions secured a valuation of $15.65 billion after its shares jumped 18.5% in their debut on Thursday, ​signaling solid investor demand for companies tied to AI-driven data ‌center growth.

item.title: Russia-linked Grinex crypto exchange suspends operations after cyber attack
item.description: Russia-linked sanctioned crypto exchange ​Grinex said on Thursday it had suspended ‌operations after assets worth 1 billion roubles ($13.10 million) were stolen during a cyber attack.

item.title: USTR's Greer says offshoring, rules of origin on agenda for Mexico trade talks
item.description: U.S. businesses continue to offshore operations to Mexico despite ​the revamped regional trade pact that was meant to stymie ‌that activity, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Thursday, and the product rules of origin underlying the deal will be the focus of talks in ​Mexico next week.

item.title: Arxis pops on Nasdaq debut, signals strong demand for aerospace IPOs
item.description: Aerospace and defense parts maker Arxis' shares surged nearly ​36% in their Nasdaq debut on ‌Thursday, signaling growing appetite for defense and industrial listings as geopolitical tensions escalate.

item.title: Fed's Miran may scale back rate-cut outlook again due to 'less favorable' inflation developments
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran, by far the U.S. central bank's most aggressive ​advocate for fast and immediate interest rate cuts, said on Thursday he ‌may again scale back the pace at which he thinks rates should fall because inflation is proving more persistent than expected.

item.title: Schroders shareholders approve $13.4 billion sale to Nuveen
item.description: Schroders shareholders voted to ​approve a 9.9 billion ‌pound ($13.4 billion) sale of the British company ​to U.S. ​rival Nuveen on Thursday, confirming ⁠the end ​of independence for one ​of London's historic fund houses.

item.title: Fed chair nominee Warsh's disclosures 'not in compliance,' US Senator Warren says
item.description: U.S. Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren on ​Thursday said she met ‌with Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh ahead ​of his scheduled confirmation ​hearing next Tuesday and ⁠believes the holes ​in his financial disclosures don't ​pass muster with Senate ethics rules and mean that ​hearing should be ​delayed.

item.title: CVS distribution center workers plan to go on strike
item.description: More than 500 CVS Health drivers and warehouse workers at the healthcare conglomerate's Fredericksburg, Virginia distribution ​center have voted to authorize a strike on ‌May 1, the International Brotherhood of Teamsters said on Thursday.

item.title: Gucci-owner Kering aims to launch luxury Google glasses next year, CEO says
item.description: Kering aims to launch smart glasses under the Gucci brand in partnership with Google next year, CEO Luca de Meo told Reuters, ​becoming potentially the first major luxury brand to enter the AI-powered eyewear ‌sector.

item.title: Coty and Interparfums deny report of talks regarding Boss, Burberry perfume licenses
item.description: Coty COTY.N on Thursday said it is not in talks to sell ​any of its prestige brand licenses, ‌denying a report by luxury news site Miss Tweed that the U.S. beauty company ​was discussing selling its Hugo Boss ​and Burberry licenses to Paris-based Interparfums .

item.title: US SEC seeks comment on contentious trading database
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission said on ​Thursday it is seeking public comment ‌on whether it should comprehensively review the so-called Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT), potentially re-opening a decade-long ​dispute over the trading database.

item.title: Gucci can no longer treat China like a 'trash bin', Kering CEO says
item.description: Kering's biggest ‌brand Gucci must rebuild its position in China after years of complacency when it treated the market as a place for easy growth, with poorly located stores and an outdated retail experience, Kering CEO Luca de Meo said on ​Thursday.

item.title: Meta to raise Quest VR headset prices in US on rising component costs
item.description: Meta Platforms said on Thursday it would increase the prices of its virtual reality headsets from April 19 in the United ​States due to rising costs of memory chips.

item.title: US derivatives regulator will make rules when he is only sitting member, chairman says
item.description: Michael Selig, chair of the ​U.S. Commodity Futures Trading ‌Commission, told Congress on Thursday the agency would ​continue issuing regulations ​even though he is ⁠the five-member agency's ​only sitting commissioner.

item.title: U.S. lawmakers scale back bill targeting Chinese chipmaking
item.description: A U.S. bill to keep more chipmaking equipment from China has been scaled back, according ‌to the latest version seen by Reuters, but still includes a new countrywide restriction on ASML's deep ultraviolet (DUV) immersion lithography machines.

item.title: Boeing unveils satellite platform, targets 26 deliveries in 2026
item.description: Boeing and its unit Millennium Space Systems are working together to ​expand production capacity and launch a ‌new satellite platform as they look to fulfill a growing backlog of orders, ​the aerospace firms said on ​Thursday.

item.title: Bank of England says it is testing AI risks to financial system
item.description: The Bank of England is testing the risks to the financial system caused by AI by conducting scenario analysis and simulations, the central bank said in a letter ​published by lawmakers on Thursday.

item.title: Myseum takes flight after Allbirds in fresh AI rebrand wave
item.description: A day after footwear maker Allbirds rose 582% on its plan to "pivot its business to AI compute infrastructure," social media firm Myseum was up 181% early ​on Thursday in the latest sign of an investor mania for artificial intelligence stocks.

item.title: US manufacturing output dips in March
item.description: U.S. factory production unexpectedly fell in March after two straight months of solid gains, weighed down by decreases in the output of motor vehicles and a range of other ​goods.

item.title: Starlink user growth accelerates as SpaceX eyes public market debut, report says
item.description: Starlink, the satellite internet unit of Elon ‌Musk's SpaceX, is seeing a surge in global user growth and app downloads, market research firm Apptopia said in a report, highlighting its role in supporting the parent's expected listing this summer.

item.title: AmEx to buy Altman-backed Hyper in push for AI-powered expense tools
item.description: Credit card giant American Express said on Thursday it would buy Hyper, an artificial intelligence-focused expense ​management startup backed by OpenAI Chief Executive Sam ‌Altman.

item.title: Google, Pentagon discuss classified AI deal, the Information reports
item.description: Alphabet's Google is negotiating an agreement ​with the Department of Defense that would ‌allow the Pentagon to deploy its Gemini AI models in classified settings, the Information reported on Thursday, citing ​two people with direct knowledge of ​the discussions.

item.title: TPG to invest $100 million in student mobility company Zum
item.description: Private equity firm TPG has ​made a $100 million investment in Zum, in a deal that values ‌the student transportation company at around $1.7 billion, Zum told Reuters on Thursday.

item.title: US weekly jobless claims decline as labor market remains stable
item.description: The number of Americans filing claims for unemployment benefits fell last week, suggesting labor market conditions remained stable, though employers are cautious about hiring new workers as ​the conflict in the Middle East casts a shadow over the economy.

item.title: Fed's Williams says war is already driving up inflation pressures
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said Thursday that the Middle East ​war is already driving up inflationary pressures, in remarks that noted the central bank is in position to respond ‌to whatever the economy might deliver.

item.title: Charles Schwab posts record quarterly profit on client growth, trading haul
item.description: Brokerage Charles Schwab posted record first-quarter profit on Thursday, lifted by robust client growth and trading ​activity.

item.title: Stellantis, Microsoft sign five-year partnership for AI push
item.description: Stellantis and ​Microsoft said on Thursday they agreed to a five‑year strategic partnership to ‌co-develop artificial intelligence (AI), cybersecurity and engineering capabilities, as the automaker races to keep pace with technology-focused rivals.

item.title: Abbott trims 2026 profit forecast on Exact deal hit, shares fall
item.description: Abbott cut its full-year profit forecast on Thursday due to ​a 20 cent-per-share hit from its recent $23 billion acquisition of cancer ‌test maker Exact Sciences, sending its shares down more than 4% premarket.

item.title: U.S. Bancorp profit jumps on interest income, fee revenue boost
item.description: U.S. Bancorp reported a 13.6% jump in first-quarter profit on Thursday, driven by higher interest income and fee revenue.
